title: Muster review-gate verdict array
description: The single-sourced shape of .muster/verdicts.json (backlog item structured-output-binding): what plugin/skills/review-gate/SKILL.md's reviewer-dispatch instructions write, and what src/review.js's tallyReview reads. Every array element is either an ordinary reviewer verdict (a named reviewer's severity-tagged findings) or a worker-absence entry (PR #82 tally-worker-exhaustion-contract: a reviewer that never produced a real verdict, recorded as a named status instead of synthesized findings) -- never both at once. tallyReview additionally tolerates several shapes OUTSIDE this schema (a missing reviewer name, an unrecognized status/severity value, findings present alongside status) as a defensive parsing fallback for a malformed real-world emission -- see src/review.js's own header comment and the exhaustion-status-producer incident. That runtime tolerance is deliberate and does not loosen what THIS schema defines as a well-formed emission; test/verdict-schema.test.js pins the one-directional coherence (every schema-valid array is accepted by tallyReview, not the reverse).

oneOf.description: An ordinary reviewer verdict: a named reviewer's severity-tagged findings.

reviewer.description: The reviewer's name, as recorded by review-gate/SKILL.md step 1's selection.

findings.description: Zero or more findings, each severity-tagged per muster-reviewer.md's vocabulary.

severity.description: The only severities src/review.js's tallyReview tallies into counts/blockers.

note.description: The finding's location, problem, and fix, per muster-reviewer.md's one-line format.

oneOf.description: A worker-absence entry (PR #82 tally-worker-exhaustion-contract): the named reviewer's dispatch was killed/exhausted mid-review or never started, so no real verdict exists to record.

reviewer.description: The reviewer's name, as recorded by review-gate/SKILL.md step 1's selection.

status.description: "exhausted": the dispatch started but was killed/ran out of budget before verdicting. "absent": the dispatch never started or never responded.
